prompt_variant: You are a critical academic research verifier. Your task: determine if a bibliog...
_search_results:
**Yes, the paper exists.**

**Exact title:** In two minds: dual-process accounts of reasoning[1][4][5][6].  
**Author:** Jonathan St. B. T. Evans (also cited as Evans, J. St. B. T. or Jonathan St BT Evans)[1][4][5][6].  
**Venue:** *Trends in Cognitive Sciences* (abbreviated *Trends Cogn Sci*)[1][4][5].  
**Year:** 2003 (October issue, volume 7, issue 10, pages 454-459)[1][4][5].  
**DOI:** 10.1016/j.tics.2003.08.012[4].  

The paper reviews dual-process theories of reasoning, proposing **two cognitive systems**: **System 1** (fast, automatic, evolutionarily older, shared with animals) and **System 2** (slow, deliberative, abstract, linked to working memory and intelligence). It argues these systems compete for control of inferences and actions, supported by experimental evidence[1][4]. PubMed lists the abstract confirming this content[4].
